bands.name: 160m
bands.notes: Shared with LORAN. 1.800-1.810 MHz Extra only CW. LSB voice below 2 MHz per convention.

bands.name: 80m
bands.notes: 3.500-3.525 Extra only CW, 3.525-3.600 CW/Data, 3.600-3.700 General phone, 3.700-4.000 all license phone. LSB voice convention.

bands.name: 60m
bands.notes: Five discrete channels: 5.3305, 5.3465, 5.357, 5.3715, 5.4035 MHz. USB voice, 60m WRC-23 expanded. Primary users have priority.

bands.name: 40m
bands.notes: 7.000-7.125 CW/Data only, 7.125-7.300 phone. LSB voice convention. Shared with shortwave broadcast outside ITU Region 2.

bands.name: 30m
bands.notes: WARC band. No phone emissions. Secondary allocation - must not interfere with fixed services.

bands.name: 20m
bands.notes: 14.000-14.150 CW/Data, 14.150-14.350 phone. USB voice convention. Primary HF DX band.

bands.name: 17m
bands.notes: WARC band. 18.068-18.100 CW/Data, 18.100-18.168 phone. USB voice convention.

bands.name: 15m
bands.notes: 21.000-21.150 CW/Data, 21.150-21.450 phone. USB voice convention. Good daytime band.

bands.name: 12m
bands.notes: WARC band. 24.890-24.920 CW/Data, 24.920-24.990 phone. USB voice convention. Often closed during solar minimum.

bands.name: 10m
bands.notes: 28.000-28.300 CW/Data, 28.300-28.600 phone, 28.600-29.700 all modes including FM. USB voice convention. FM repeater segment 29.5-29.7 MHz.

notes: Based on ARRL Band Plan and FCC Part 97 regulations. License classes shown are US allocations. LSB voice convention below 10 MHz, USB above 10 MHz.
